name: Tour around the Natural Park of l'Albufera from Valencia
name_original: Vuelta al Parque Natural de l'Albufera desde Valencia
region_intro: Road cycling in Valencia offers diverse terrain, ranging from flat coastal plains to challenging mountainous interiors. The region features an extensive network of bike lanes, dedicated cycle paths, and natural parks. Notable areas include the Turia Gardens, the flat routes of Albufera Natural Park, and the mountainous landscapes of Sierra Calderona and Serra Perenxisa. This variety provides options for different cycling preferences and skill levels. Best road cycling routes in Valencia The most popular road cycling route is El Monduver — Circular from Gandia through La Drova and Barx ports, a 37.2 miles (59.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gains through mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tour around the Natural Park of l'Albufera from Valencia, a moderate 53.3 miles (85.8 km) path. This route offers flat terrain through the protected wetland and rice paddies of Albufera Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the Serra Calderona Climbs Loop, a 52.8 miles (84.9 km) trail leading through the mountainous Sierra Calderona Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling in Valencia is defined by coastal paths, extensive natural parks, and mountainous regions with significant climbs. The network offers options for both leisurely rides and advanced challenges. The routes in Valencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1,500 reviews. More than 100,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Valencia?
A: Valencia offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find extensive flat coastal plains, ideal for leisurely rides, as well as challenging mountainous interiors with significant elevation gains. Notable areas include the flat, scenic routes through &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839571609/tour-around-the-natural-park-of-l-albufera-from-valencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the demanding climbs in regions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843182221/serra-calderona-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Calderona&lt;/a&gt; and Serra Perenxisa.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Valencia?
A: Valencia enjoys a Mediterranean climate with around 300 days of sunshine annually, making it suitable for cycling year-round. The most pleasant periods for road cycling are typically from early May to mid-July and mid-September to mid-October, when temperatures are mild and comfortable. Winters are generally mild, while summers can be warm, especially in the midday sun.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Valencia?
A: Yes, Valencia offers many options for less experienced riders and families. The flat coastal paths and the car-free &lt;a href="https://www.visitvalencia.com/en/what-to-do-valencia/city-routes/valencia-by-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Gardens&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for easy, scenic rides. The routes through &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839571609/tour-around-the-natural-park-of-l-albufera-from-valencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; are also predominantly flat and well-maintained, offering a relaxed cycling experience through beautiful landscapes. Komoot lists over 340 easy road cycling tours in the region.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Valencia?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking climbs, the mountainous interior of Valencia province is ideal. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843182221/serra-calderona-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Calderona Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is particularly popular, featuring mythical mountain passes like El Pico del Águila, Chirivilla, and the famous Oronet. Other areas like Serra Perenxisa also offer challenging ascents and varied scenery, often utilized in professional cycling events.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see along road cycling routes in Valencia?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839457779/el-monduver-circular-from-gandia-through-la-drova-and-barx-ports" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Monduver circular route&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic vistas from mountain passes. In the city, the Turia Gardens connect the historic center with the futuristic City of Arts and Sciences. For elevated perspectives, consider routes that pass by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2439037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Valencia?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Valencia are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839457779/el-monduver-circular-from-gandia-through-la-drova-and-barx-ports" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Monduver — Circular from Gandia&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843182221/serra-calderona-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Calderona Climbs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839698228/gallinera-valley-and-la-llacuna-loop-from-gandia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley and La Llacuna Loop from Gandía&lt;/a&gt;. These loops are popular for exploring diverse landscapes without retracing your path.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Valencia?
A: The road cycling routes in Valencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1,500 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs, as well as the extensive network of bike lanes and the beautiful natural parks like Albufera and Sierra Calderona.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Valencia on komoot?
A: There are over 1,900 road cycling routes available in Valencia on komoot, catering to all skill levels. This includes nearly 350 easy routes, almost 1,000 moderate routes, and over 580 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Valencia?
A: Yes, Valencia offers several long-distance road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839571609/tour-around-the-natural-park-of-l-albufera-from-valencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Natural Park of l'Albufera from Valencia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 85.8 km path. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859615356/tour-of-the-interior-of-la-safor-la-costera-and-la-vall-d-albaida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the interior of La Safor, La Costera and La Vall d'Albaida&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 81 km with significant elevation.

Q: Can I find bike-friendly amenities like cafes or accommodation near road cycling routes?
A: Valencia is a bike-friendly region, and you'll often find cafes and other amenities along popular cycling routes, especially in towns and villages. The city itself has numerous options, and routes through areas like Albufera Natural Park often pass through villages like El Palmar, known for its traditional Valencian paella restaurants. Many accommodations also cater to cyclists.

Q: What are the options for parking or public transport access to road cycling trails in Valencia?
A: Within Valencia city, there's an extensive network of bike lanes, making it easy to access urban routes like the Turia Gardens. For routes outside the city, many starting points in towns and villages offer parking. Public transport, particularly regional trains, can also be an option for reaching certain areas, though it's advisable to check specific regulations for bikes on public transport.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks ideal for road cycling in Valencia?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839571609/tour-around-the-natural-park-of-l-albufera-from-valencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers flat, serene routes through wetlands and rice paddies, perfect for all levels. For more challenging rides, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843182221/serra-calderona-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Calderona Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is renowned for its mountainous terrain and breathtaking views. Both parks provide a dramatic backdrop of diverse ecosystems for your ride.
